hints.text: Plates with **red text** — visible through the blur
White bollards w/ **yellow rectangular front reflector** (white back); dark-brown bollard w/ 2 red stripes (also NL)

shared_with.note: Similar white bollards — but with red stripe + non-rectangular reflector.
The brown 2-red-stripe bollard exists there too.

hints.text: A-type guardrails w/ **yellow reflectors** (Europe: only ES/AD/BE)
Concrete **square poles w/ tiny holes** + small metal wrappings (wrappings also in France); second type w/ oval holes
Directional signs blue or white; **small BLUE sub-signs** under road signs (other countries: white); km markers green (sometimes red text)
hints.uniq_note: The blue sub-sign is the quick Belgium tell — neighbours use white ones.

hints.text: Pedestrian sign: **two horizontal dotted lines + square-headed man** (FR/NL: 5-stripe zebra)
Chevrons **white w/ red arrow** (also NL, never FR)

shared_with.diff: Red plate text + concrete roads → Belgium.

hints.text: Rural roads often of **concrete slabs** (PH/TH-like; NL almost never); all-white lines; bike lanes both sides w/ **short dashed outer lines**; **gutters on both sides of almost every road** (rare next door)
hints.uniq_note: The gutters + concrete slabs combo is the fastest NL-vs-BE separator.

shared_with.note: Concrete slab roads are extremely common there too.
shared_with.diff: Gutters both sides + European context → Belgium.

shared_with.note: Concrete roads with a large square pattern are common.
shared_with.diff: Gutters both sides + European context → Belgium.

hints.text: **Red-brick houses** (N+W); south = grey stone, French look; **2nd-most blurred houses** after Germany

**Orange signposts/sign backs** — unique to Belgium; street signs short & tall, often w/ **municipality name** (NL signs long & low); town entry = white w/ black border + bold name; priority signs = small orange diamonds; bus stops = **yellow posts** + yellow shelter roofs
Traffic lights on **black-yellow or red-white striped poles**; red **H hydrant signs** (also Germany)
Rare red car w/ long antenna (much commoner in Ukraine); Gen4 smallcam
**3 languages**: Dutch/Flemish (z, w, ee, ij) → Flanders (north); French (ç é è à) → Wallonia (south); German (ä ü ö ß) → Ostbelgien; **Brussels bilingual FR+NL**

**Flanders vs Wallonia**: bus-stop top white (Flanders/Brussels) vs **yellow** (Wallonia); warning signs thick red border w/o white rim (Flanders, = Spanish style) vs w/ white rim (Wallonia); traffic-light poles **yellow-black = Flanders only**, red-white = Wallonia/Brussels
**Area codes**: 1–2 digits after the 0 (04 = mobile); big-city street-sign designs learnable (Bruges doesn't name the municipality!)
Flat NW (coast) → hillier SE; **Ardennes** = steep hills, dense forest, valleys (spills into FR/LU/DE); coastal plain = polders (NL-confusable!); coast lined by an apartment **'wall'** + dunes
**Baarle-Hertog** (NE of Antwerp): BE/NL enclave patchwork — borders marked with **white crosses**, street signs reveal the country
